pi_items.verified_value: ct_cranium/0051 - Official InVesalius v3.0 sample DICOM dataset (0051.zip)
pi_items.source: GitHub API query to https://api.github.com/repos/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/tags/v3.0 shows 0051.zip asset published 2017-04-10, 25,545,646 bytes, 8,743 downloads. Setup script confirms source: https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip
setup_task.sh line 10 defines OUTPUT_DIR=/home/ga/Documents/tissue_exports. Verified in export_result.sh line 13.
export_result.sh line 14 defines inv3_path. Verified in README.md and verifier.py.
export_result.sh line 17, README.md line 19, verifier.py line 101-110
export_result.sh line 18, README.md line 20, verifier.py line 114-123
export_result.sh line 19, README.md line 21, verifier.py line 127-136
README.md specifies 3 tissue types (full bone, compact cortical bone, soft tissue). Verifier.py line 30 retrieves this from metadata. Export result checks for ≥3 masks.
README.md specifies 3 STL exports. Task description lists 3 required exports. Verifier logic validates 3 separate STL files.
README.md line 23 states 'min_stl_triangles': 1000. Not explicitly used in verification scoring but mentioned in metadata. Export_result.sh parses triangle_count from STL files.

pi_items.verified_value: ≥226 HU is within established CT segmentation standards. Literature shows bone thresholds vary by application: 200-500 HU for spongy bone, ≥500 HU for cortical bone. Value 226 captures all bone tissue including trabecular bone.
pi_items.source: README.md line 14 specifies 'Full bone tissue mask (Hounsfield threshold minimum must be at or above 226 HU)'. Medical imaging literature from ScienceDirect and Nature shows similar bone threshold ranges. Export_result.sh line 92-93 validates masks with thresh[0] >= 226 and thresh[1] >= 1000 as bone masks.

pi_items.verified_value: ≥662 HU is within established cortical bone range. Literature indicates adult compact cortical bone is typically 500-2000 HU. Value 662 selects dense cortical bone.
pi_items.source: README.md line 15 specifies 'Compact cortical bone mask (Hounsfield threshold minimum at or above 662 HU)'. Medical imaging literature indicates cortical bone at 500-1000+ HU. Export_result.sh line 95 identifies compact bone masks where thresh[0] >= 662.

pi_items.verified_value: ≤225 HU is within established soft tissue range. Literature shows soft tissue typically ranges from -50 to +199 HU or -50 to +200 HU depending on scanner protocol. Max 225 captures standard soft tissue.
pi_items.source: README.md line 16 specifies 'Soft tissue mask (Hounsfield threshold maximum at or below 225 HU)'. Medical imaging literature from ScienceDirect shows soft tissue at -50 to 199 HU. Export_result.sh line 98 identifies soft tissue masks where thresh[1] <= 225.

pi_items.verified_value: Real medical imaging DICOM dataset. The CT Cranium 0051 file has been the official InVesalius sample dataset since at least 2001 (CTI Renato Archer Center, Brazil Ministry of Health). Currently distributed via GitHub v3.0 release (April 2017) and invesalius.github.io/download.html. Not synthetic - real de-identified patient CT scan.
pi_items.source: GitHub API confirms v3.0 release 2017-04-10 with 0051.zip. Official download page invesalius.github.io lists 'Cranium' as one of 6 test DICOM samples. CTI Renato Archer Center context indicates clinical dataset usage. No evidence of synthetic generation - consistent with real medical imaging data.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official InVesalius CT Cranium DICOM sample dataset (0051), a real de-identified medical imaging dataset distributed via GitHub v3.0 release (April 2017) and the official InVesalius download page. The dataset contains a complete cranial CT scan. All output file paths (/home/ga/Documents/tissue_exports/) and file names (bone_tissue.stl, compact_bone.stl, soft_tissue.stl, tissue_analysis.inv3) are correctly specified. Three segmentation masks are required with Hounsfield thresholds: full bone (≥226 HU), compact cortical bone (≥662 HU), and soft tissue (≤225 HU). These thresholds are consistent with established medical imaging CT segmentation standards based on literature values for tissue classification. The minimum STL triangle count requirement is 1000 triangles per mesh.
